notes: Gemini CLI is the IN-PLACE rewind variant (like claudecode), not the fork variant (codex). /rewind opens the RewindViewer picker; on confirm, ChatRecordingService.rewindTo() slices the in-memory messages and APPENDS a single {"$rewindTo":"<id>"} marker to the same append-only chats/session-*.jsonl (it never truncates the file). The watched chats file keeps its header sessionId, so the daemon keeps the SAME session_id. The parser's ParseLine skips the $rewindTo line (default arm → ev.Skip=true: no $set, no type), so the rewind is invisible to irrlicht's surface — no transcript_removed/new, no session change. That is fine: the spec doesn't require detecting the rewind, only that state stays correct after one. Three user-observable signals, all emitted: (1) the session stays ready across the rewind (a $rewindTo marker is not a turn) — asserted as post_rewind_ready; (2) the SAME session_id is retained (gemini rewinds in place; same_session_as on v3) — the in-place signal; (3) the post-rewind turn ('three') drives a clean ready→working→ready arc from the rewound point. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live default boot model and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it (a different live default is a string update, not a daemon bug). Caveat (recorded in assessment): context-utilization % may overstate after a rewind because the parser counts all appended-transcript tokens while gemini only re-feeds the post-rewind subset; cumulative cost stays accurate. DRIVER GAP (keys): the /rewind picker needs arrow+Enter and the gemini-cli driver stubs `keys` (driver-interactive.sh:236) — record ports the keys arm before driving.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch (pre-session or chats sessionId, whichever arrives first)
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working
Second turn completes back to ready — checkpoint 2, the point to rewind back to

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after /rewind the session stays in ready (the rewind appends a $rewindTo marker the parser skips; it is not a turn). Gemini rewinds in place on the same session_id (no fork, no new file).

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — third prompt ('three') drives ready → working on the SAME session_id (gemini rewinds in place rather than forking to a new session).
Spec: third turn completes back to ready on the same session — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
